In the home every convenience is
provided. Bathroom, hot and cold
water, and a hot water heating sys-
tenn The two daughters and the
mother do all the housework, yet in
the busy harvest season, with a
dozen men to cook for, everything
was kept in good order with no APP
parent effort


In the parlor is a piano, and games
of various sorts which the hired men
are urged to use each evening after
the days work is over, and which
they usually do. One of Mr.. Spence's
principles Is that no man shall be
kept on the place who is unfit for
his family to associate with. This
| is a farm where orders prevails
everywhere. The buildings are all IN
| good repair, machinery is stored away
! when not in use, everything is kept
in tiptop condition all over the farm
In the wheat field the stacks of grain
are placed so as to be in straight
rows. This is also a farm of profits
Mr.. Spence'S share of the presen


wheat crop will be about 10000 bush
els. Beside this he raises and ha:


for sale so good cattle every year
and 100 hogs. This season the gros'


income will not be far from $4,000


ana the net income about $500. Thu


entire farm is valued at 100000, an'


is the product of IS years of diligen


and intelligent effort
